title: Brooke
content: Brooke is an international charity that protects and improves the lives of horses, donkeys and mules which give people in the developing world the opportunity to work their way out of poverty.
snippet: Brooke is an international charity that protects and improves the lives of horses, donkeys and mules which give people in the developing world the opportunity to work their way out of poverty.

title: Heat adaptive architecture design competition: Meet the winners

title: Why resilience and disaster management are central to jobs
content: Resilience is an investment in the conditions that make jobs possible.
snippet: Resilience is an investment in the conditions that make jobs possible.

title: ‘Vanishing Acts’: Missing women and girls as an invisible crisis in post-disaster landscapes
content: Research conducted between 2024 and 2025 across six police jurisdictions in the Indian Sundarbans revealed that over 900 people were reported missing in the aftermath of the cyclones Amphan and Yaas. Most of them were women and young girls.
snippet: Research conducted between 2024 and 2025 across six police jurisdictions in the Indian Sundarbans revealed that over 900 people were reported missing in the aftermath of the cyclones Amphan and Yaas.

title: Climate experts say spring is coming earlier. How will that affect agriculture and ecosystems?
content: An earlier spring affects when migratory birds arrive, leaves emerge, and fruit ripens — among plants and animals that determine ecosystem health.
snippet: An earlier spring affects when migratory birds arrive, leaves emerge, and fruit ripens — among plants and animals that determine ecosystem health.

title: As climate change threatens student athlete safety, states try to adapt
content: Rising extreme heat in the U.S. is putting student athletes at risk, exposing inequalities in school resources and driving uneven adaptation efforts across states.
snippet: Rising extreme heat in the U.S. is putting student athletes at risk, exposing inequalities in school resources and driving uneven adaptation efforts across states.

title: Advancing private sector climate readiness in Central Asia
content: Expanding environment, social, and governance tools and due diligence practices can help firms better manage exposure to climate risks.
snippet: Expanding environment, social, and governance tools and due diligence practices can help firms better manage exposure to climate risks.

title: Landsat reveals reservoir changes and bathymetry
content: Researchers from the University of Southampton used Landsat data to identify where water advanced or retreated from 1984 to 2022, creating the first global dataset pinpointing the exact year of permanent surface water changes.

title: Mental health impacts of climate-related hazards among farmers: evidence from a community-based study

title: Climate change and extreme heat: strengthening resilience and adaptive capacity in the Southern African development community (SADC)

title: Academy of Science of South Africa (ASSAf)

title: Extreme heat from climate change linked to smaller babies
content: Exposure to extreme heat conditions is resulting in more babies being born with low birth weight, according to a new study involving Adelaide University researchers.
snippet: Exposure to extreme heat conditions is resulting in more babies being born with low birth weight, according to a new study involving Adelaide University researchers.

title: The platform that wants to save Pakistan’s groundwater
content: The Groundwater Management Information System is bringing data, institutions and farmers together to save a resource at risk.
snippet: The Groundwater Management Information System is bringing data, institutions and farmers together to save a resource at risk.

title: 2 trillion gallons of water trigger historic flooding in Hawaiʻi
content: More than 2 trillion gallons of water—enough to fill 3 million Olympic-sized swimming pools—inundated Hawaiʻi in March.
snippet: More than 2 trillion gallons of water—enough to fill 3 million Olympic-sized swimming pools—inundated Hawaiʻi in March.

title: UNEP FI makes climate scenario data accessible and actionable for financial institutions

title: Who pays the price for Japan’s nuclear power?
content: Because Fukushima is considered a national responsibility, it is legally stipulated that all contaminated soil must be removed from the prefecture by 2045.
snippet: Because Fukushima is considered a national responsibility, it is legally stipulated that all contaminated soil must be removed from the prefecture by 2045.

title: Fifth of arabica coffee growing area to be unsuitable by 2050, report says

snippet: Climate change could result in 20% of current arabica coffee growing areas being classified as unsuitable by 2050, Rabobank forecast in a report on Monday.

title: Pocket gardens: The tiny urban oases with surprisingly big benefits

title: Winter’s alarmingly low snowpack offers a glimpse of the changing rhythm of water in the western US

title: We have the proof that logging makes Tasmania’s forests more flammable
